mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 3 months signals monetary easing that lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20-day SPX return remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals an emerging labor market deterioration that historically precedes earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, measured across all available triggers.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising CPIAUCSL inflation alongside a stable or rising FEDFUNDS rate implies a positive and rising real policy rate, which tightens financial conditions and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. Neither series alone captures this effect; the interaction of nominal rate minus inflation trend is required.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the FEDFUNDS-minus-CPIAUCSL-trend real rate proxy against forward 20-day returns fails to exceed 0.03 in absolute value over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening regime (FEDFUNDS trending up over 6 months), a simultaneous decline in HOUST signals interest-rate-sensitive housing demand contraction that leads broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. Outside tightening regimes this housing signal has no predictive power because low rates offset weak permits.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising trend over 6 months AND HOUST declines > 5% over 3 months
failure_condition: In tightening regimes, SPX 60-day forward returns following the joint trigger are not significantly more negative than baseline tightening-period returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in RSAFS reflects resilient consumer spending that supports near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in stronger consumption data.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS month-over-month change and forward 5-day SPX return stays below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO exceeding 0.5% month-over-month signals an industrial production contraction that historically precedes broader earnings weakness in cyclical sectors, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days once the threshold is breached.
failure_condition: SPX shows non-negative average 20-day forward returns following triggered INDPRO declines across at least two distinct business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A Sahm-rule-like joint signal where UNRATE rises while PAYEMS growth decelerates or turns negative indicates an accelerating labor market downturn entering or within a recessionary regime, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. Neither series individually reliably signals recession onset without the other confirming direction.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month moving average rises > 0.4pp above 12-month low AND PAYEMS month-over-month change turns negative
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly more negative than unconditional baseline returns following the joint trigger, tested via t-test p > 0.05 across historical triggers.

mechanism_narrative: In an expansionary regime where GDPC1 growth remains positive quarter-over-quarter, continued real GDP growth confirms sustained corporate earnings capacity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. In contractionary or stagnant regimes this relationship inverts or disappears because GDP growth becomes a lagging confirmation rather than a leading signal.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth and forward 60-day SPX return falls below 0.03 during any expansionary period lasting more than 4 quarters.
